skill.name: longbridge-earnings
skill.description: Provides pre-earnings previews and post-earnings analysis for US, Hong Kong, and A-share stocks using Longbridge data. It can produce a fast in-chat summary or a full Markdown research report with valuation work.
skill.summary: Analyze company earnings with Longbridge market data, consensus estimates, and structured report templates.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk alerts, but review shows most are false positives from Markdown code fences, relative links, and financial-analysis text. The confirmed risk is intended execution of the Longbridge CLI through a Python helper, plus networked market-data retrieval and temp-file output. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Intended External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The helper script runs the Longbridge CLI with subprocess.run to collect market data. It uses an argument list without shell=True, which lowers command-injection risk, but marketplace users should understand that the skill can execute a local external command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run usage is present and semantically central to the skill. The command is fixed to longbridge and does not use a shell, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Networked Market Data Collection
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to Longbridge CLI and MCP data sources and supplements with web search for transcripts or consensus history. This is expected for an earnings research skill, but it means results depend on external services and user authentication state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly reference Longbridge CLI, Longbridge MCP, and web search. The network use matches the stated financial-data purpose and shows no evidence of exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Temporary Market Data Files
low_findings.description: The collector writes raw CLI responses and error files under a temp directory named from the ticker. This is useful for full-report reuse, but users should be aware that local market-data artifacts may remain after analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File writes are explicit and limited to collected Longbridge output. No evidence found of sensitive filesystem reads or arbitrary path writes.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak-crypto, Windows SAM, path-traversal, and many Ruby backtick alerts are false positives from Markdown syntax, ordinary financial text, and relative links to sibling skills. No evidence found that these lines implement cryptography, access the SAM database, run Ruby code, or traverse the filesystem.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these are documentation or prose contexts, not executable code. The suspicious tokens are incidental strings such as Markdown backticks, SAME, and ../ links.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: scripts/collect.py invokes subprocess.run with the longbridge executable and user-provided symbol as an argument. The lack of shell=True avoids direct shell expansion, but the external command pattern remains security-relevant.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact subprocess.run call is present and executes an external binary. The argument-vector form limits injection evidence, so the pattern is confirmed but bounded.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Temp Directory Output Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The script creates a ticker-derived directory in the system temp location and stores raw JSON or error output there. This is not malicious, but it is a filesystem side effect that marketplace users should understand.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The temp directory behavior is explicit and connected to report reuse. The reviewed code does not show arbitrary path traversal or sensitive file collection.

content.value_statement: Earnings analysis often requires pulling consensus, financial statements, price data, and transcripts from many places. This skill organizes Longbridge data into fast previews, post-earnings summaries, and full research reports.
actual_capabilities: Creates pre-earnings previews with prior guidance, recent events, call Q&A, and key watch items.
Builds post-earnings summary cards covering beat or miss, segments, margins, guidance, and Street views.
Runs a Python helper to collect Longbridge CLI data into a compact digest for analysis.
Supports full Markdown research reports with DCF, trading comps, and optional precedent transaction work.
Handles US, Hong Kong, and A-share tickers when Longbridge data is available.
Matches the response language to the user while keeping tickers, filenames, and metrics in English.
limitations: Requires the Longbridge CLI or configured Longbridge MCP access for the strongest data coverage.
May need web search for earnings call transcripts, whisper numbers, or M&A transaction details.
Does not guarantee investment outcomes and should not be treated as financial advice.
Full reports depend on current data quality, fiscal-period detection, and available filings.

use_cases.title: Prepare for an Upcoming Earnings Release
use_cases.description: Review prior guidance, recent events, analyst questions, and the main metrics to watch before results arrive.

use_cases.title: Summarize Results After the Print
use_cases.description: Turn Longbridge data into a concise post-earnings card with KPI variance, segment trends, margin changes, and risks.

use_cases.title: Draft a Full Earnings Research Note
use_cases.description: Produce a Markdown report with earnings analysis, estimate revisions, valuation math, and rating rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Earnings Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick post-earnings view.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the latest earnings for TSLA.US and give me the default summary card.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Earnings Preview
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare before a company reports results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a pre-earnings preview for 700.HK. Focus on prior guidance, recent events, and the top questions to watch.

prompt_templates.title: Full Research Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Request a deeper Markdown report after results are available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a full earnings report for AAPL.US with beat or miss analysis, segments, margins, guidance, valuation, and rating rationale.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Valuation Update
prompt_templates.scenario: Update an investment view after new results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze 600519.SH after earnings and update the valuation using DCF, trading comps, and a bear-base-bull range. Show the rating decision.

output_examples.output: A compact earnings card with the detected quarter, core KPI table, segment revenue mix, margin trend, thesis status, Street view, next-quarter consensus, and key risks.

output_examples.input: Create a pre-earnings preview for NVDA.US.
output_examples.output: An inline preview covering prior guidance fulfillment, management outlook, prior call Q&A, recent events, guidance reliability, consensus gaps, focus points, and risk flags.

output_examples.input: Generate a full report for TSLA.US after earnings.
output_examples.output: A Markdown research report with earnings summary, revenue analysis, segment review, profitability, guidance, estimates, valuation, scenarios, thesis update, risks, and sources.

best_practices: Provide the exact ticker and market suffix so the data collector can query the correct listing.
Specify preview, summary card, or full report to control depth and output format.
Review fiscal quarter, release date, and source dates before relying on valuation conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for licensed financial advice or portfolio suitability review.
Do not request fabricated numbers when Longbridge or public sources have missing data.
Do not mix different reporting periods when comparing guidance, consensus, and actual results.

faq.question: Does this skill make investment recommendations?
faq.answer: It can draft rating language inside a research workflow, but users must treat the output as analysis, not financial advice.

faq.question: What data sources does it use?
faq.answer: It primarily uses Longbridge CLI or MCP data, with web search for transcripts and supplemental public information when needed.

faq.question: Can it analyze pre-earnings and post-earnings situations?
faq.answer: Yes. It has separate workflows for upcoming earnings previews and results that have already been reported.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The default output is an in-chat summary. Full analysis is delivered as a Markdown research report.

faq.question: Which markets are covered?
faq.answer: The skill is designed for US, Hong Kong, and A-share tickers when Longbridge has the needed data.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs a local Longbridge CLI helper and writes temp files. Review found no malicious intent or credential exfiltration.
